commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f3afa272c34f3a4799aa18c7ffd09a8698595a20)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes provider auth checks and visible model catalog filtering to async per-provider work with event-loop yields, and adds auth-profile failure listeners to invalidate prepared provider-auth state.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was performed in this read-only review. Source inspection confirms current main has a synchronous per-provider auth-filter path and the PR body provides before logs, but after-fix Discord proof is still missing.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label, is stacked on an open base PR, lacks contributor real behavior proof, and needs the async migration defect resolved before normal merge review.

target.title: perf(models): make provider auth checks non-blocking
target.body:
## Summary

Two changes to make the provider auth check survive Discord's 3 s ack deadline and to keep the prepared auth map honest after auth state changes outside the gateway.

**1. Non-blocking auth checks.** When the prepared map misses (startup window, scope override), `buildModelsProviderData` falls back to per-provider compute over ~30–40 providers, each doing sync fs reads + external-CLI probes (~500 ms each). The whole sweep ran on the event loop thread, blocking Discord's 3 s ack heartbeat. `hasAuthForModelProvider` is now a real async function with a `setImmediate` yield before the slow path; a sweep over N providers releases the event loop between providers so Discord acks land on time while the catalog-auth filter runs. Total sync CPU time is unchanged — the leaves (`fs.readFileSync`, `execSync` in `auth-profiles/store.ts`) are still sync; converting those is a much bigger refactor left for a follow-up.

**2. Self-heal on observed auth failures.** ClawSweeper flagged that removing the TTL leaves the prepared map stale if auth state changes outside the gateway's own reload/logout paths (token rotation, OAuth revoke, external `codex login`). New listener registered on `markAuthProfileFailure` clears the prepared map on any observed failure — next call recomputes against the real auth state. No TTL, no polling, no fs watcher; just hook the event the gateway already raises when an auth call fails.

## Evidence — before

`/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-21.log`, slow `/models` calls when the prepared map misses (per-provider compute path):

```
14:38:39  buildModelsProviderData done in 22941ms preparedAuth=false agentId=main
14:39:14  buildModelsProviderData done in 22610ms preparedAuth=false agentId=main
14:39:44  buildModelsProviderData done in 22814ms preparedAuth=false agentId=main
14:40:17  buildModelsProviderData done in 22825ms preparedAuth=false agentId=main
```

Matching gateway liveness diagnostic during one of those calls:

```
14:44:14 [diagnostic] liveness warning: reasons=event_loop_delay,event_loop_utilization,cpu
  interval=30s
  eventLoopDelayP99Ms=24461.2
  eventLoopDelayMaxMs=24461.2
  eventLoopUtilization=0.987
  cpuCoreRatio=1
  active=1 waiting=0 queued=0
  work=[active=agent:main:discord:direct:1499113075284901960 (processing, q=1, age=28s)]
```

The 24.5 s blocked event-loop window lines up with the ~22.9 s `buildModelsProviderData` runtime. Discord's 3 s ack deadline runs on the same event loop, so the deferral never gets sent and the interaction fails.

## Stack

This PR is stacked on top of #85125 (the prepared-state prewarm). Merge that one first; this one rebases cleanly onto main once it lands.

## Changes

**Async auth checks**

- `src/agents/model-provider-auth.ts`:
  - `hasAuthForModelProvider` → `async` / `Promise<boolean>`.
  - `await new Promise<void>((r) => setImmediate(r))` inserted before the slow path.
  - `createProviderAuthChecker` returns an async closure.
  - New helper `resolveProviderAuthMap({ providers, ... })`: resolves a known provider set up-front via the async checker, returns a sync `(provider) => boolean` closure. Use when handing off to existing sync helpers.
  - Warmer's internal call awaits per-provider.
- `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.ts`: `resolveVisibleModelCatalog` is async; internal `.filter(...)` becomes a `for…of` with `await hasAuth(entry.provider)`.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts`: awaits `resolveVisibleModelCatalog`; the catalog auth-filter loop awaits per provider.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/models.ts`: awaits `resolveVisibleModelCatalog`.
- `src/flows/model-picker.ts`: three sites switched from `createProviderAuthChecker` to `resolveProviderAuthMap` over the relevant provider set — keeps the wizard's option-builder helpers sync.

**Self-heal on auth-profile failure**

- `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts`: new `AuthProfileFailureEvent` type; module-local listener registry; `registerAuthProfileFailureListener` / `clearAuthProfileFailureListeners` exports; `markAuthProfileFailure` fires the listeners on both code paths (lock-based update and in-memory fall-through). Listener errors are swallowed so they can't break failure recording.
- `src/agents/auth-profiles.ts` (barrel): re-exports the new symbols.
- `src/agents/model-provider-auth.ts`: new `wirePreparedAuthInvalidationToAuthFailures()` that registers `clearCurrentProviderAuthState` as a listener; returns the unregister function for tests.
- `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts`: calls `wirePreparedAuthInvalidationToAuthFailures()` at startup, right before the prewarm.
- `src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts`: new test asserting that firing the listener clears the prepared map and forces recompute on the next read.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts` — 6/6 passing (5 original + 1 new self-heal case)
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts` — 11/11 passing (listener registry doesn't regress existing behavior)
- [x] Local build (`./run.sh build`) clean (exit 0)
- [ ] Restart gateway with the branch; trigger `/models` from Discord during the warmer window (~50 s after startup) to exercise the slow path; confirm Discord ack does not time out and the liveness warning's `eventLoopDelayP99` drops well below the previous ~24 s
- [ ] Trigger `/models` again post-warm to confirm the fast path still returns sub-10 ms
- [ ] Trigger an auth failure (e.g., revoke a token mid-session) and confirm the next `/models` call computes against the real auth state instead of returning the stale prepared answer
